A landspout developed Monday afternoon as a sea breeze boundary interacted with additional thunderstorm boundaries. Emergency management indicated minor damage to a couple of homes along with a downed power pole near the intersection of Marco Ave and Beard St in DeSoto county. Winds are estimated around 50-60 mph with a path length of less than a quarter of a mile.

Winds are estimated around 50-60 mph with a path length of less than a quarter of a mile.

Both broadcast media and Emergency Management shared images and reports of a landspout over the Pinellas County landfill. This landspout was observed underneath a developing updraft devoid of any precipitation. No damage was reported by emergency management. Time is estimated based upon reporting time and radar.

A SKYWARN spotter sent in a video of roof damage to a bank drive-thru on Regency Park Boulevard in Port Richey. Video shows the roof deck has been pulled back and roofing material is scattered in the parking lot.
